title: Transport Poverty in Freight Transportation - Engineering Challenges, Impacts, and Strategic Solutions for Improved Economic Connectivity
venue: PhD Thesis, Warsaw University of Technology, Faculty of Transport, 2028
abstract: This research examines transport poverty in freight transportation, focusing mainly on how infrastructure limitations, accessibility gaps, and logistical inefficiencies affect economic connectivity. The study analyzes engineering challenges, evaluates system-level impacts on supply chains and regional development, and proposes strategic solutions to improve freight accessibility. A combination of analytical and simulation-based approaches is used to assess network performance and identify areas of intervention.
